description_long: This is a classic claw hammer featuring a robust, slightly weathered wooden handle and a metallic head. The head has a flat striking face on one side and a curved, bifurcated claw on the other, designed for pulling nails. The metal head shows signs of rust and wear, indicating frequent use, while the handle has a dark, aged patina. The hammer's proportions are typical for hand tools used in construction or repair, with a handle long enough for a firm grip and leverage. The overall appearance suggests durability and functionality, suitable for urban environments such as garages, workshops, or construction sites. No visible branding or additional attachments are present.
description: A well-used claw hammer with a dark wooden handle and a rusted metal head, featuring a flat striking face and a curved claw for nail removal.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the profile of the metal head with its pronounced claw.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow width of the handle and the split in the claw section of the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the hammer, with the claw and striking face visible.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view showing the end of the handle and the flat face of the hammer head.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle with a dark, aged finish and a slightly textured surface from wear
attribute_head_type: claw hammer head with a flat striking face and a curved, split claw for nail removal, made of steel
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible rust, scratches, and wear on the metal head; handle shows signs of aging and use
